NAME

server.c - WebSocket server implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct websocket_connection_data_t
Per-connection user data.

Functions


asciichat_error_t websocket_server_init (websocket_server_t *server, const websocket_server_config_t *config)
asciichat_error_t websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t *server)
void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t *server)
void websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t *server)

Detailed Description

WebSocket server implementation.

void websocket_server_cancel_service (websocket_server_t * server)

void
  websocket_server_destroy (websocket_server_t * server)


Definition at line 810 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


810                                                           {
811   if (!server) {
812     return;
813   }
814 
815   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, false);
816 
817   // Cancel any pending libwebsockets service calls to interrupt blocking lws_service()
818   // This prevents the event loop from getting stuck in lws_service()
819   if (server->context) {
820     log_debug("[WEBSOCKET_SERVER_DESTROY] Cancelling libwebsockets service");
821     lws_cancel_service(server->context);
822   }
823 
824   // Unregister atomic fields
825   NAMED_UNREGISTER(&server->running);
826 
827   // Destroy handler thread pool (waits for pending work to complete)
828   if (server->handler_pool) {
829     thread_pool_destroy(server->handler_pool);
830     server->handler_pool = NULL;
831   }
832 
833   // Context is normally destroyed by websocket_server_run (from the event loop
834   // thread) for fast shutdown. This handles the case where run() wasn't called
835   // or didn't complete normally.
836   if (server->context) {
837     NAMED_UNREGISTER(server->context);
838     log_debug("WebSocket context still alive in destroy, cleaning up");
839     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
840     server->context = NULL;
841   }
842 
843   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
844 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
    thread_pool_destroy().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t
  websocket_server_init (websocket_server_t * server, const
  websocket_server_config_t * config)


Definition at line 676 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


676                                                                                                              {
677   if (!server || !config || !config->client_handler) {
678     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters");
679   }
680 
681   memset(server, 0, sizeof(*server));
682   server->handler = config->client_handler;
683   server->user_data = config->user_data;
684   server->port = config->port;
685   atomic_store_bool(&server->running, true);
686 
687   // Store server pointer in protocol user data so callbacks can access it
688   // Both the HTTP and ACIP protocols need access to the server
689   websocket_protocols[0].user = server; // http protocol
690   websocket_protocols[1].user = server; // acip protocol
691 
692   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
693   lws_log_init_server();
694 
695   // Configure libwebsockets context
696   struct lws_context_creation_info info = {0};
697   info.port = config->port;
698   info.protocols = websocket_protocols;
699   info.gid = (gid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
700   info.uid = (uid_t)-1;                                // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
701   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT; // Initialize SSL/TLS support (required for server binding)
702   info.extensions = NULL;                              // Disable permessage-deflate - causing connection issues
703   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &keep_alive_policy; // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
704 
705   // Configure TLS/WSS support if certificates are provided (check for non-empty strings)
706   if (config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0' && config->tls_key_path &&
707       config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0') {
708 #ifdef LWS_WITH_TLS
709     info.ssl_cert_filepath = config->tls_cert_path;
710     info.ssl_private_key_filepath = config->tls_key_path;
711     log_info("WebSocket server configured for WSS (TLS): cert=%s, key=%s", config->tls_cert_path, config->tls_key_path);
712 #else
713     log_warn("WebSocket server: TLS support not compiled in libwebsockets; WSS unavailable");
714 #endif
715   } else if ((config->tls_cert_path && config->tls_cert_path[0] != '\0') ||
716              (config->tls_key_path && config->tls_key_path[0] != '\0')) {
717     log_warn("WebSocket server: Both TLS certificate and key must be provided for WSS; using plain WS");
718   }
719 
720   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
721   // Default is 4KB, causing large frames to fragment into many chunks
722   // Video frames are 921KB, so increase to 2MB to handle them without fragmentation
723   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 2 * 1024 * 1024; // 2MB per-thread service buffer
724 
725   // Disable ALL default timeouts and keep-alive mechanisms
726   // Use only the explicit retry_and_idle_policy we set above (30/35 seconds)
727   info.ka_time = 0;           // Disable TCP keep-alive probes (use WebSocket pings instead)
728   info.ka_probes = 0;         // Disable TCP probes
729   info.ka_interval = 0;       // Disable TCP probe intervals
730   info.keepalive_timeout = 0; // Disable HTTP keep-alive timeout entirely
731 
732   // Explicitly set a very large idle timeout to prevent early disconnection
733   // Without this, libwebsockets defaults to 5 seconds for HTTP connections
734   // Set to effectively infinite (100 hours) since we use retry_and_idle_policy instead
735   info.timeout_secs = 360000; // 100 hours - effectively disabled
736 
737   // Create libwebsockets context
738   server->context = lws_create_context(&info);
739   if (!server->context) {
740     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_BIND, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
741   }
742 
743   // Create thread pool for handling client connections
744   // Use 4 worker threads to handle concurrent client handlers without blocking LWS event loop
745   server->handler_pool = thread_pool_create_with_workers("websocket_handlers", 4);
746   if (!server->handler_pool) {
747     lws_context_destroy(server->context);
748     server->context = NULL;
749     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to create WebSocket handler thread pool");
750   }
751 
752   /* Register WebSocket server with named registry */
753   char ws_port_name[32];
754   snprintf(ws_port_name, sizeof(ws_port_name), "ws:%d", server->port);
755   NAMED_REGISTER(server, ws_port_name, "websocket_server", "0x%tx", NULL);
756 
757   /* Register server's sync primitives with hierarchical naming */
758   NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&server->running, "is_running", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
759 
760   /* Register WebSocket context as child of server */
761   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET(server->context, "context", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(server));
762 
763   log_info("WebSocket server initialized on port %d with static file serving", config->port);
764   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
765 }






References atomic_store_bool(),
    lws_log_init_server(), and
  thread_pool_create_with_workers().


Referenced by acds_main(), and server_main().






asciichat_error_t
  websocket_server_run (websocket_server_t * server)


Definition at line 767 of file
    lib/network/websocket/server.c.


767                                                                    {
768   if (!server || !server->context) {
769     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid server");
770   }
771 
772   log_info("WebSocket server starting event loop on port %d", server->port);
773 
774   // Run libwebsockets event loop
775   uint64_t last_service_ns = 0;
776   int service_call_count = 0;
777   while (atomic_load_bool(&server->running)) {
778     // Service libwebsockets with 16ms timeout to match 60 FPS frame rate.
779     // This provides frequent event processing (~60 callback invocations per second) so that
780     // WebSocket frames queued by the render loop are sent promptly, matching TCP performance.
781     // All client connections share this single server context, so a single lws_service() call
782     // services fragments and events for all clients simultaneously.
783     uint64_t service_start_ns = time_get_ns();
784     if (last_service_ns && service_start_ns - last_service_ns > 30 * US_PER_MS_INT) {
785       // > 30ms gap between service calls
786       double gap_ms = (double)(service_start_ns - last_service_ns) / 1e6;
787       log_info_every(1 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE_GAP] %.1fms gap between lws_service calls", gap_ms);
788     }
789     service_call_count++;
790     log_debug_every(500 * US_PER_MS_INT, "[LWS_SERVICE] Call #%d, context=%s", service_call_count,
791                     NAMED_DESCRIBE(server->context, "websocket_server"));
792     int result = lws_service(server->context, 1);
793     if (result < 0) {
794       log_error("libwebsockets service error: %d", result);
795       break;
796     }
797   }
798 
799   log_info(
800       "WebSocket server event loop exited (context will be destroyed by main thread after handler threads complete)");
801   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
802 }






References atomic_load_bool(), and
  time_get_ns().
